danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring evasion or defeat through indirect means

danger_signals.visual_form: Enclosed room with boss sprite and throwable objects
danger_signals.meaning: Mandatory combat encounter; player must use environmental objects to damage boss
Fall damage or death; requires grappling hook or board placement to cross safely
Reduced visibility; candle collectible required to navigate and spot hazards
Progression blocked; requires key item, puzzle solution, or enemy defeat

safety_signals.meaning: Protective shield against one hit of damage; accumulation grants extra lives
Extra life gain; increases player resilience
Continue item; allows progression persistence after total party wipe

safety_signals.visual_form: Both Goofy and Max visible on screen simultaneously
safety_signals.meaning: Safer play state; multiplayer synergy enables puzzle solutions and enemy crowd control

safety_signals.visual_form: Clear, well-lit rooms with few or no animated threats
safety_signals.meaning: Safe area for item collection, puzzle solving, and regrouping

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Disney character designs; cooperative pair dynamic
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Beach fishing trip starting location; home island of Spoonerville

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Pirate ship aesthetic; maritime stronghold architecture
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Generic pirate enemies throughout levels; Keelhaul Pete as final boss with distinct pirate captain design
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Progression from civilian areas (beach, village) to pirate-controlled zones (haunted castle, cavern, pirate ship); Pete and PJ held captive on ship
Village level showing civilian settlement under pirate threat; Pete mistaken for pirate captain creates false peace with captors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Well-lit areas, visible platforms, collectible items scattered throughout, ambient enemy presence
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrol slowly or remain stationary; players can collect items freely; puzzles are interactive but non-threatening

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemies become more animated and aggressive; room may shake slightly on collision
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies move toward players; players must dodge, throw objects, or activate traps to defeat threats

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dramatically reduced visual field; black or very dark blue screen
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player movement continues but visibility severely limited; hazard avoidance becomes guesswork until candle is used

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Isolated arena with boss sprite, limited exit options, throwable objects arranged in room
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Boss executes attack patterns; players must time object throws; arena may have secondary hazards (alligator pit for final boss)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visually sealed doors, ice walls, or enemy blockades; visual cues (sliding blocks, pressure plates) indicate solution requirements
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Progression impossible until puzzle solved; bell item may lure enemies to reveal traps; multiple players required for simultaneous activation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pete and PJ visible in pirate ship level; Keelhaul Pete revealed as distinct final boss; alligator hazard introduced
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rescue mechanics required; players must navigate to Pete/PJ location; final boss fight unavoidable for story conclusion

environmental_storytelling.example: Game progresses from civilian beach → village under siege → haunted castle → cavern → pirate ship stronghold, visually conveying how deep players penetrate enemy territory and narrative escalation toward Keelhaul Pete
Pete and PJ positioned on pirate ship in final level, visible in boss arena context; alligator pit beneath them serves dual purpose: environmental hazard + visual statement of their peril

environmental_storytelling.technique: Boss identity reveal through sprite design
environmental_storytelling.example: Keelhaul Pete's final boss sprite differs from Pete's standard sprite, immediately communicating mistaken identity twist when players encounter the 'real' pirate captain
Candle-dependent sections force players to recognize environmental barriers and resource management; visual darkness itself communicates 'forbidden or dangerous space'
Locked gates and ice walls suggest recent pirate fortification; sliding blocks and pressure plates show improvised civilian defenses or ancient cavern mechanisms

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Moves at 320 sp/f horizontally/vertically (slower than Max); takes longer to pick up items (11 frames for pot pickup, 72 frames for general items); 27-frame block kick
Deals 5 damage per thrown pot (higher damage output than Max); slower but more powerful, indicating 'tank' role in cooperative play
Moves at 384 sp/f horizontally/vertically (fastest base speed); faster item pickup (13 frames for pot, 74 frames for general items); 28-frame block kick
Deals 4 damage per thrown pot (lower damage); faster movement compensates, indicating 'scout/agility' role
9-frame stun on taking damage; encourages rapid repositioning after hit to avoid follow-up attacks
Travels 4 pixels per frame in both directions; 3-frame hold = 9-frame total freeze (3 forward + 3 return + 3 wait); incentivizes careful timing to avoid immobility
Can be knocked off stages, lured with bells, trapped in throwable paths, or defeated by blocks/bombs; never engage in direct combat with players
Execute preset attack patterns; require thrown object hits to damage; typically require 4+ hits to defeat; arena-specific hazards may assist player
Positioned above alligator pit; defeat triggers rescue sequence and game conclusion; visual and mechanical emphasis on 'ultimate threat' status

notes: Goof Troop communicates world-state primarily through location progression and visual environment design rather than explicit HUD indicators. The game uses cooperative multiplayer as a core manifestation mechanic: puzzles visually require two players (simultaneous block placement, pressure plates), making single-player feel 'incomplete' despite technical playability. Danger signals rely on enemy sprite animation and environmental hazards rather than color coding. The pirate faction is established through maritime architecture escalation and NPC placement rather than faction insignia or color identity. Character stat differences (Goofy slower/stronger vs. Max faster/weaker) are communicated purely through gameplay response, not visual tells. The story twist (Keelhaul Pete's return) is conveyed through a sprite-swap reveal in the final arena, making visual identity crucial to narrative comprehension.
